Guarding My Heart in Doctrinal Conversations

Previous Baptism for the Boys

It is easy, especially after studying weak or unbiblical theories of the atonement, to become dismissive or irritated. Some modern theories can sound shallow, man-centered, or logically inconsistent. However, Scripture calls us not only to defend truth, but to do so with humility and gentleness.

Date: 24 Feb 2026
Series: Devotion
Tags:
DoctrineTheologyHumility
Status: published

A Personal Discipline for Defending Truth with Humility

Why This Matters

Section titled “Why This Matters”

It is easy, especially after studying weak or unbiblical theories of the atonement, to become dismissive or irritated. Some modern theories can sound shallow, man-centered, or logically inconsistent. However, Scripture calls us not only to defend truth, but to do so with humility and gentleness.

“Contend earnestly for the faith which was once for all handed down to the saints.” (Jude 1:3)
“The Lord’s bond-servant must not be quarrelsome… correcting those who are in opposition with gentleness.” (2 Timothy 2:24–25)
“Speaking the truth in love.” (Ephesians 4:15)

Truth matters. But tone matters too.

A Short Personal Discipline Before Speaking

Section titled “A Short Personal Discipline Before Speaking”

1️⃣ Pause — Remember Who Opened Your Eyes

Section titled “1️⃣ Pause — Remember Who Opened Your Eyes”

Pray:

“Lord, if I understand anything rightly, it is because You showed me.”

“What do you have that you did not receive?” (1 Corinthians 4:7)
“By the grace of God I am what I am.” (1 Corinthians 15:10)

This kills pride immediately.

2️⃣ Re-center — My Goal Is Not to Win

Section titled “2️⃣ Re-center — My Goal Is Not to Win”

Pray:

“My goal is not to win an argument, but to honor Christ.”

“Let all that you do be done in love.” (1 Corinthians 16:14)
“Speaking the truth in love.” (Ephesians 4:15)

Winning arguments is flesh. Winning souls is spiritual.

3️⃣ Ask for the Right Spirit

Section titled “3️⃣ Ask for the Right Spirit”

Pray:

“Give me firmness without harshness.”

“The Lord’s bond-servant must not be quarrelsome… correcting with gentleness.” (2 Timothy 2:24–25)
“Let your speech always be with grace.” (Colossians 4:6)

Gentleness is controlled strength.

4️⃣ Stay Anchored in Scripture

Section titled “4️⃣ Stay Anchored in Scripture”

Commit beforehand:

  • I will argue from Scripture.
  • I will avoid sarcasm.
  • I will avoid labeling.
  • I will avoid dismissive tone.

If I find myself thinking,
“This is just nonsense,”
that is my cue to slow down.

5️⃣ Remember What Sovereign Grace Produces

Section titled “5️⃣ Remember What Sovereign Grace Produces”

If I believe:

  • Salvation is sovereign
  • Hearts are changed by the Spirit
  • Eyes are opened by God

Then I do not need to force anything.

“The anger of man does not achieve the righteousness of God.” (James 1:20)

Calm theology should produce calm demeanor.

A 30-Second Prayer Before Conversations

Section titled “A 30-Second Prayer Before Conversations”

“Father, You opened my eyes by grace. Guard me from pride. Let me defend truth without harshness. Help me love the person in front of me more than I love being right. Make my words clear, biblical, and gentle. I trust You to change hearts. Amen.”

A Conversation Template for Real-Life Discussions

Section titled “A Conversation Template for Real-Life Discussions”

When someone says:

  • “Everyone has the ability to choose God.”
  • “Everyone will have a chance to choose God”

Instead of correcting immediately, use this pattern:

Step 1 — Clarify

Section titled “Step 1 — Clarify”

“Help me understand what you mean by ability or given a chance?”

Step 2 — Affirm What You Can

Section titled “Step 2 — Affirm What You Can”

“I agree that everyone is responsible to repent and believe.”

Step 3 — Gently Introduce Scripture

Section titled “Step 3 — Gently Introduce Scripture”

“How do you understand this verse?”

  • “The mind set on the flesh is hostile toward God; for it does not subject itself to the law of God, for it is not even able to do so.” (Romans 8:7)
  • “No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me draws him.” (John 6:44)

Let Scripture do the heavy lifting.

Step 4 — Keep It Relational

Section titled “Step 4 — Keep It Relational”

“I’m not trying to argue — I genuinely want to understand how you reconcile that.”

Tone determines whether the conversation continues.

Step 5 — Leave Room for the Spirit

Section titled “Step 5 — Leave Room for the Spirit”

You do not have to close the case.

You can say:

“That’s something I’ve wrestled with for a long time. I’d encourage you to meditate on those passages.”

Trust God to work.

Final Reminder

Section titled “Final Reminder”

The doctrines of grace should produce:

  • Humility
  • Patience
  • Gentleness
  • Confidence in God

If they produce arrogance, something has gone wrong in the heart.

Strong doctrine.
Soft tone.
Firm convictions.
Gentle spirit.

That combination reflects Christ.
